summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that setting the a11y-first-slide-message HTML attribute on a <swiper-container> custom element to a value containing an img tag with an onerror handler results in arbitrary JavaScript execution when the user presses Enter on a navigation button. The attacker-controlled attribute value flows unsanitized from HTML attribute parsing through to innerHTML injection via the a11y notify() function.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the crafted a11y-first-slide-message attribute value to be parsed by get-element-params.mjs, flow into the a11y module's notify() function, be injected into the DOM via setInnerHTML when the user presses Enter on the prev navigation button, and execute the attacker-controlled onerror JavaScript handler.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Swiper library, served an exploit page with a malicious a11y-first-slide-message attribute in a headless browser, simulated keyboard interaction (focusing the prev navigation button and pressing Enter), and confirmed that the attacker-controlled JavaScript executed. The onerror handler set a proof-of-compromise variable (window.__xss_proof), and the verification script confirmed the variable was set to true.
verification_method: The verify.js script uses Puppeteer to load the exploit page in headless Chromium, waits for Swiper initialization, simulates keyboard interaction by dispatching a keydown event (Enter) on the prev navigation button inside the Shadow DOM, then evaluates whether the window.__xss_proof variable was set by the onerror handler. This distinguishes a real XSS from a failed interaction or unrelated DOM change.
evidence_produced_by_run: The headless browser loaded the exploit page containing .
Swiper initialized and the a11y module set up keyboard event listeners on the navigation buttons.
The verification script dispatched a keydown event (keyCode 13, Enter) on the prev navigation button.
The onEnterOrSpaceKey handler called notify(params.firstSlideMessage), which called setInnerHTML with the malicious HTML.
The img element's onerror handler executed the proof() function, setting window.__xss_proof to true.
The verification script confirmed window.__xss_proof === true, proving arbitrary JavaScript execution.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref (v12.1.1), builds the Swiper library, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
